title: Golden Globes 2026 Snubs & Surprises: 'Hamnet,' 'Sinners' & More
text: Golden Globes 2026 delivered several major surprises. Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet unexpectedly won Best Motion Picture – Drama, defeating the favored Sinners. Zhao appeared visibly shocked upon winning the ceremony's final award. She did mention Sinners director Ryan Coogler in her acceptance remarks. Teyana Taylor claimed Best Female Supporting Actor for One Battle After Another, surprising industry observers. Amy Madigan from Weapons had been positioned as the frontrunner after earning Critics Choice recognition. Frankenstein left empty-handed despite five Golden Globe nominations and a Critics Choice Award win for Jacob Elordi by Guillermo del Toro. The Netflix film failed to resonate with voters. Wicked: For Good failed to secure any awards despite stars Ariana Grande and Colman Domingo's attendance. The sequel to the previous year's successful Wicked surprised no one given the shift in voter mood. Bugonia, Yorgos Lanthimos' sci-fi comedy with Emma Stone, despite actor nominations, didn't resonate with voters despite the director's previous success at the Golden Globes. One Battle After Another swept directing and screenplay honors, preventing the anticipated vote split between the two Warner Bros. contenders.
